tables.name: AccessConfig
tables.description: Controls which tools and entity types are available for use, and which departments can access each one. Admin tier users on the Tools page bypass department filtering and see all enabled items. Non-admin users see only items explicitly granted to their department.
tables.dataFlow: Rows are manually inserted to grant department-level access to specific tools and entity types. The BDL Import entity picker queries this table filtered by the logged-in user's department scope to determine which entity types to display. Admin tier users skip this check entirely and see all enabled entities from the catalog. Future tool types (CDL, Payment, API) use the same table with different tool_type values.

columns.name: config_id
columns.description: Identity primary key.

columns.name: tool_type
columns.description: Identifies the tool or pipeline this access grant applies to. Values include BDL, CDL, PAYMENT, NEWBUSINESS, API, and other tool identifiers as they are added. No check constraint — values are open to accommodate future tools.

columns.name: item_key
columns.description: Specific item within the tool type that access is being granted to. For BDL and CDL, this is the entity_type from the corresponding catalog table (e.g., PHONE, CONSUMER_TAG). NULL for tools that do not have sub-item granularity.

columns.name: department_scope
columns.description: Department key this access grant applies to. Matches RBAC_DepartmentRegistry.department_key. Rows should always have a department_scope populated — unrestricted access is handled by admin tier bypass, not by NULL scope rows.

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Whether this access grant is active. 0 = disabled, 1 = enabled (default).

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When the access grant was created.

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who created the access grant.

columns.name: modified_dttm
columns.description: When the access grant was last modified.

columns.name: modified_by
columns.description: Who last modified the access grant.

designNotes.note: Users with admin tier on the Tools page see all enabled entities regardless of AccessConfig rows. This table only filters non-admin users. The Applications team receives admin tier on the BDL Import page, so no AccessConfig rows are needed for them. Only departments with restricted access (e.g., Business Intelligence) require rows.

A NULL item_key means access to the tool type itself without sub-item granularity — used for tools like Drools Refresh that have no entity picker. A NULL department_scope would mean unrestricted access to all departments, but this is not the expected pattern — admin tier handles unrestricted access. Rows should always have a department_scope populated.

queries.name: Available BDL entities for a department
queries.description: Returns the BDL entity types accessible to a specific department. Join to Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry for display names and field details.
queries.sql:
SELECT ac.item_key AS entity_type, f.type_name, f.folder, f.element_count
FROM Tools.AccessConfig ac
INNER JOIN Tools.Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry f
    ON f.entity_type = ac.item_key
    AND f.spec_version = '11.1.0.1.6'
WHERE ac.tool_type = 'BDL'
    AND ac.department_scope = 'business-intelligence'
    AND ac.is_active = 1
ORDER BY f.folder, f.entity_type;

queries.name: All access grants by tool type
queries.description: Shows the complete access matrix across all departments and tool types.

relationshipNotes.note: Logical relationship. department_scope matches RBAC_DepartmentRegistry.department_key. No physical foreign key — validated at the application layer, consistent with the RBAC_RoleMapping pattern.
Logical relationship. For BDL tool_type rows, item_key corresponds to Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry.entity_type. No physical foreign key since AccessConfig spans multiple tool types and catalogs.

Parent table. AccessFieldConfig rows provide a field-level whitelist for department-scoped entity grants. When AccessFieldConfig rows exist for a config_id, only those fields are accessible to the department. No AccessFieldConfig rows means zero field access (strict whitelist). Admin tier users bypass both tables entirely.

tables.name: AccessFieldConfig
tables.description: Whitelist of BDL element fields accessible to a department for a specific entity type. Child of AccessConfig — each row grants access to one field within the parent entity grant. Admin tier users bypass this table entirely. When an AccessConfig row exists but has no AccessFieldConfig children, the department has zero field access to that entity (strict whitelist). Fields not in this table are invisible to the department in the column mapping UI.
tables.dataFlow: Rows are manually inserted when granting a department access to specific fields within a BDL entity type. The BDL Import entity-fields API endpoint queries this table for non-admin users, returning only fields that have an active whitelist row for the user's department. Admin tier users skip this check and see all visible fields from the catalog. The config_id foreign key links to AccessConfig, which provides the entity type and department scope context.

columns.name: field_config_id
columns.description: Identity primary key.

columns.name: config_id
columns.description: FK to Tools.AccessConfig. Identifies the parent entity-level access grant this field whitelist belongs to.

columns.name: element_name
columns.description: BDL element name being granted. Matches Catalog_BDLElementRegistry.element_name.

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Whether this field grant is active. 0 = disabled, 1 = enabled (default).

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When the field grant was created.

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who created the field grant.

columns.name: modified_dttm
columns.description: When the field grant was last modified.

columns.name: modified_by
columns.description: Who last modified the field grant.

relationshipNotes.note: Child table. config_id references AccessConfig.config_id. Each AccessFieldConfig row grants access to one element within the entity and department defined by the parent AccessConfig row. No child rows means zero field access (strict whitelist).

element_name references element names from Catalog_BDLElementRegistry. No formal FK — the element catalog may be reloaded independently. Validation is enforced at the application layer during field access queries.

tables.name: BDL_ImportLog
tables.description: Audit trail for BDL import executions. One row per import capturing the full lifecycle from file upload and validation through XML construction, DM file registration, and import submission. Tracks who executed each import, against which environment, for which entity type, and what the outcome was.
tables.dataFlow: A row is inserted when a user initiates a BDL import from the Control Center, initially with VALIDATING status. The status column is updated as the import progresses through the lifecycle: VALIDATING (data checks running), BUILDING (XML file being constructed), REGISTERED (file registered with DM via POST /fileregistry), SUBMITTED (import triggered via POST /fileregistry/{id}/bdlimport), COMPLETED (DM confirmed successful processing), or FAILED (error at any stage). The column_mapping JSON is captured at validation time. The file_registry_id is populated after successful DM registration. Rows are append-only — status updates modify the existing row but no rows are ever deleted.

columns.name: log_id
columns.description: Identity primary key.

columns.name: server_config_id
columns.description: FK to Tools.ServerConfig. Identifies the environment and server configuration used for this import.

columns.name: environment
columns.description: Denormalized environment from ServerConfig (PROD, STAGE, TEST). Avoids joins for common queries and history views.

columns.name: entity_type
columns.description: BDL entity type imported (e.g., PHONE, CONSUMER_TAG). Matches Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry.entity_type.

columns.name: source_filename
columns.description: Original filename of the file uploaded by the user (CSV or Excel).

columns.name: xml_filename
columns.description: Name of the BDL XML file written to the dmfs import folder. NULL until the BUILDING stage completes. Used for DM file registration.

columns.name: row_count
columns.description: Number of data rows in the uploaded file. NULL until file parsing completes. Excludes header rows.

columns.name: validation_errors
columns.description: Count of rows that failed validation. 0 indicates a clean validation pass. Populated during the VALIDATING stage.

columns.name: column_mapping
columns.description: JSON representation of the field mapping used for this import. Captures which source file columns were mapped to which BDL element names. Provides a complete audit trail independent of whether a template was used.

columns.name: value_changes
columns.description: JSON array of value replacements applied by the user during the validation step. Each entry captures the field name, original value, replacement value, affected row count, who made the change, and when. NULL when no replacements were applied. Provides full audit trail from source file content to final import data.

columns.name: template_id
columns.description: FK to future template table. Identifies the saved column mapping template used for this import. NULL indicates an ad-hoc mapping was configured manually.

columns.name: file_registry_id
columns.description: File registry ID returned by the DM REST API after successful file registration (POST /fileregistry). NULL until the REGISTERED stage. Used as the path parameter for the import trigger call.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Current lifecycle status of the import. Progresses through VALIDATING, BUILDING, REGISTERED, SUBMITTED, COMPLETED, or FAILED. See status values for details.

columns.name: error_message
columns.description: Error details captured on failure. NULL when status is not FAILED. May contain DM API error responses, validation summary, or file system errors depending on which stage failed.

columns.name: executed_by
columns.description: AD username of the Control Center user who initiated the import. Captured from the authenticated session at import start.

columns.name: started_dttm
columns.description: When the import process was initiated by the user.

columns.name: completed_dttm
columns.description: When the import reached a terminal status (COMPLETED or FAILED). NULL while the import is in progress.

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When the log row was created.

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who created the log row.

designNotes.note: The column_mapping column stores a JSON representation of the field mapping used for the import — which source file columns were mapped to which BDL element names. This provides a complete audit trail of what was actually imported, regardless of whether a template was used. When a template is selected, the mapping is locked (read-only in the UI) and the template_id is also recorded.

Failed imports require a new file with a new filename to be registered with DM — re-importing a previously registered file is not supported. A retry creates a new BDL_ImportLog row rather than updating the failed row. The failed row preserves the error context for diagnostics.

The SUBMITTED to COMPLETED transition reflects DM confirming successful processing. DM updates the file registry status to complete when the import finishes. The mechanism for checking this status (polling, callback, or manual confirmation) is determined by the import workflow implementation.

statusValues.meaning: Import initiated. Uploaded file is being parsed and data validation checks are running against the column mapping.
Validation passed. The BDL XML file is being constructed from the mapped data.
XML file written to dmfs and registered with DM via POST /fileregistry. file_registry_id is now populated.
BDL import triggered via POST /fileregistry/{id}/bdlimport. Awaiting DM processing confirmation.
Import failed at any stage. error_message contains details. A retry requires a new import with a new filename.

queries.name: Recent import history
queries.description: Shows the most recent BDL imports across all environments with key details.
queries.sql:
SELECT TOP 50 log_id, environment, entity_type, source_filename,
    row_count, validation_errors, status, error_message,
    executed_by, started_dttm, completed_dttm
FROM Tools.BDL_ImportLog
ORDER BY log_id DESC;

queries.name: Failed imports requiring attention
queries.description: Shows failed imports that may need retry or investigation.
queries.sql:
SELECT log_id, environment, entity_type, source_filename,
    row_count, status, error_message,
    executed_by, started_dttm
FROM Tools.BDL_ImportLog
WHERE status = 'FAILED'
ORDER BY started_dttm DESC;

queries.name: Import activity by user
queries.description: Shows import counts and outcomes per user for auditing.
queries.sql:
SELECT executed_by, environment, entity_type,
    COUNT(*) AS total_imports,
    SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'COMPLETED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS completed,
    SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS failed
FROM Tools.BDL_ImportLog
GROUP BY executed_by, environment, entity_type
ORDER BY executed_by, environment;

relationshipNotes.note: Parent table. server_config_id references ServerConfig.config_id. Identifies which environment and server configuration was used for the import. The environment column is denormalized from ServerConfig for convenience.

template_id is reserved for a future column mapping template table. When templates are implemented, this FK will link to the saved template used for the import. NULL indicates an ad-hoc mapping was used.

tables.name: Catalog_ApiRegistry
tables.description: REST API endpoint catalog containing one row per path and HTTP method combination. Parsed from OpenAPI 3.0 YAML specification files. Supports multi-product cataloging via product_name column. Links to Catalog_ApiSchemaRegistry via request_schema and response_schema for field-level detail.
tables.dataFlow: Populated by a Python parsing script that reads OpenAPI 3.0 YAML specification files and generates INSERT statements. Consumed by modules that need to discover available API endpoints for automation features.

columns.name: endpoint_id
columns.description: Identity primary key.

columns.name: spec_version
columns.description: OpenAPI spec version identifier. No default constraint — every insert must explicitly specify.

columns.name: product_name
columns.description: Source product name. Enables future multi-product cataloging.

columns.name: resource_tag
columns.description: OpenAPI tag identifying the resource group this endpoint belongs to.

columns.name: endpoint_path
columns.description: URL path template with placeholders for path parameters.

columns.name: http_method
columns.description: HTTP verb: GET, POST, PUT, or DELETE.

columns.name: operation_id
columns.description: OpenAPI operationId. Not guaranteed unique — some operations share the same ID across different paths.

columns.name: summary
columns.description: Short one-line summary from the spec.

columns.name: description
columns.description: Full description from the spec. May contain HTML markup.

columns.name: operation_type
columns.description: Classified CRUD type derived from operationId patterns during import: CREATE, RETRIEVE, UPDATE, DELETE, SEARCH, or ACTION.

columns.name: request_content_type
columns.description: Request MIME type. NULL for endpoints with no request body.

columns.name: request_schema
columns.description: Schema name for the request body model object. Links to Catalog_ApiSchemaRegistry. NULL when no request body.

columns.name: response_content_type
columns.description: Response MIME type for successful responses. NULL for 204 No Content responses.

columns.name: response_schema
columns.description: Schema name for the successful response model object. Links to Catalog_ApiSchemaRegistry.

columns.name: response_is_array
columns.description: Whether the successful response returns an array of the schema type.

columns.name: path_params
columns.description: Comma-separated list of path parameter names.

columns.name: query_params
columns.description: Comma-separated list of query parameter names.

columns.name: path_param_count
columns.description: Number of path parameters on this endpoint.

columns.name: query_param_count
columns.description: Number of query parameters on this endpoint.

columns.name: is_deprecated
columns.description: Whether the endpoint is marked deprecated in the spec.

columns.name: api_version
columns.description: FICO API version number extracted from the content type. Values 1 through 4 observed in current spec.

designNotes.note: The OpenAPI spec reuses the same operationId across different paths. For example, saveImage appears on four paths (accounts, consumers, creditors, receivers). The unique constraint is on spec_version + endpoint_path + http_method, not on operation_id. Queries filtering by operation_id should expect multiple rows.

The operation_type column is derived during import by pattern-matching the operationId: create/add/save/assign/import map to CREATE, retrieve/get/list/find map to RETRIEVE, update/modify map to UPDATE, delete/remove/unassign map to DELETE, search maps to SEARCH, and everything else maps to ACTION. ACTION captures non-CRUD operations like status updates, batch triggers, and workflow actions.
The product_name column enables cataloging APIs from multiple products in the same table. Queries should always filter on product_name and spec_version to scope results to a specific product release.

The request_schema and response_schema columns contain model object names that link to Catalog_ApiSchemaRegistry.schema_name. This is a string-based link, not a foreign key, because the relationship is many-to-many (multiple endpoints share schemas) and some endpoints reference schemas that have no properties (service infrastructure types). Join on spec_version + schema_name for correct results.

queries.name: Endpoint count by resource group
queries.description: Overview of the API surface area showing how many endpoints exist per resource tag with CRUD breakdown.
queries.sql:
SELECT resource_tag, COUNT(*) AS endpoints,
    SUM(CASE WHEN operation_type = 'CREATE' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS creates,
    SUM(CASE WHEN operation_type = 'RETRIEVE' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS retrieves,
    SUM(CASE WHEN operation_type = 'UPDATE' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS updates,
    SUM(CASE WHEN operation_type = 'DELETE' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS deletes,
    SUM(CASE WHEN is_deprecated = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS deprecated
FROM Tools.Catalog_ApiRegistry
WHERE spec_version = '11.1.0.1.6'
GROUP BY resource_tag
ORDER BY endpoints DESC;

queries.name: Find endpoints for a resource with request/response detail
queries.description: Shows all endpoints for a given resource tag with their schemas. Replace the tag value as needed.
queries.sql:
SELECT http_method, operation_id, summary, endpoint_path, 
    request_schema, response_schema, response_is_array
FROM Tools.Catalog_ApiRegistry
WHERE resource_tag = 'tags'
    AND spec_version = '11.1.0.1.6'
    AND is_deprecated = 0
ORDER BY endpoint_path, http_method;

queries.name: Full endpoint detail with request body fields
queries.description: Joins to the schema registry to show the complete request body structure for an endpoint. Replace the operation_id as needed.
queries.sql:
SELECT r.endpoint_path, r.http_method, r.summary,
    s.property_name, s.property_type, s.ref_schema, 
    s.is_required, s.is_read_only,
    LEFT(s.property_description, 120) AS description_preview
FROM Tools.Catalog_ApiRegistry r
INNER JOIN Tools.Catalog_ApiSchemaRegistry s
    ON r.spec_version = s.spec_version
    AND r.request_schema = s.schema_name
WHERE r.operation_id = 'createConsumerAccountCase'
    AND r.spec_version = '11.1.0.1.6'
ORDER BY s.sort_order;

relationshipNotes.note: Links via request_schema and response_schema to schema_name. An endpoint's request body structure is defined by the schema matching request_schema. The response structure is defined by response_schema. When response_is_array is 1, the response returns a list of that schema type. Some endpoints have NULL for both (e.g., DELETE operations with no body and 204 responses).

tables.name: Catalog_ApiSchemaRegistry
tables.description: REST API schema property catalog containing one row per property within each model object. Parsed from the components/schemas section of OpenAPI 3.0 YAML specification files. Schema descriptions and property counts are denormalized onto each property row to avoid a third table. Links to Catalog_ApiRegistry via schema name.
tables.dataFlow: Populated by the same Python parsing script that populates Catalog_ApiRegistry, reading model object definitions from the components/schemas section of the OpenAPI YAML. Consumed by modules that need field-level detail for API request construction and response parsing.

columns.name: schema_property_id
columns.description: Identity primary key.

columns.name: spec_version
columns.description: OpenAPI spec version identifier. Must match values in Catalog_ApiRegistry.

columns.name: product_name
columns.description: Source product name. Must match values in Catalog_ApiRegistry.

columns.name: schema_name
columns.description: Model object name from the OpenAPI spec. This is the join key to Catalog_ApiRegistry request_schema and response_schema columns.

columns.name: schema_description
columns.description: Schema-level description. Denormalized — repeated on every property row for query convenience.

columns.name: schema_property_count
columns.description: Total number of properties in this schema. Denormalized — repeated on every property row.

columns.name: property_name
columns.description: JSON property name as it appears in API request and response payloads.

columns.name: property_type
columns.description: Data type: string, integer, boolean, number, array, or object. NULL when the type is a schema reference.

columns.name: property_format
columns.description: OpenAPI format qualifier such as date-time or int64. NULL when not specified.

columns.name: property_description
columns.description: Full property description from the spec. Often includes the underlying DM database column name in parentheses.

columns.name: ref_schema
columns.description: Referenced schema name when this property is a complex type or array of complex types. NULL for primitive types.

columns.name: is_array
columns.description: Whether this property is an array type.

columns.name: is_required
columns.description: Whether this property is listed in the schema required array.

columns.name: is_read_only
columns.description: Whether the property description indicates READ-ONLY. Derived during import by text matching.

columns.name: default_value
columns.description: Default value if specified in the spec.

columns.name: sort_order
columns.description: Ordinal position of this property within its parent schema. 1-based.

designNotes.note: Schema-level fields (schema_description, schema_property_count) are repeated on every property row rather than stored in a separate header table. This avoids a third table while keeping queries simple. The trade-off is repeated descriptions across all property rows for a given schema — minimal storage cost.

When a property is a complex type rather than a primitive, property_type is NULL and ref_schema contains the referenced schema name. When a property is an array of complex types, is_array is 1 and ref_schema contains the item schema. This enables traversing the full object graph by following ref_schema links recursively.

The is_read_only flag is derived by text-matching READ-ONLY in the property description during import. This identifies system-generated fields that should not be included in POST/PUT request bodies. Not all read-only fields are explicitly marked in the spec, so this is a best-effort flag.

queries.name: Schema property detail
queries.description: Shows all properties for a given schema with types and descriptions. Replace schema name as needed.
queries.sql:
SELECT property_name, property_type, ref_schema, 
    is_required, is_read_only, is_array,
    LEFT(property_description, 150) AS description_preview
FROM Tools.Catalog_ApiSchemaRegistry
WHERE schema_name = 'ConsumerAccountCaseRequestRM'
    AND spec_version = '11.1.0.1.6'
ORDER BY sort_order;

queries.name: Find schemas that reference a given schema
queries.description: Discovers which model objects contain references to a specific schema — useful for understanding the object graph.
queries.sql:
SELECT schema_name, property_name, is_array
FROM Tools.Catalog_ApiSchemaRegistry
WHERE ref_schema = 'ReferenceRM'
    AND spec_version = '11.1.0.1.6'
ORDER BY schema_name, sort_order;

queries.name: Properties containing DM column names
queries.description: Many property descriptions include the underlying DM database column name in parentheses. This query finds them for cross-referencing with crs5_oltp schema.
queries.sql:
SELECT schema_name, property_name, 
    LEFT(property_description, 200) AS description_preview
FROM Tools.Catalog_ApiSchemaRegistry
WHERE property_description LIKE '%(%_%)%'
    AND spec_version = '11.1.0.1.6'
ORDER BY schema_name, sort_order;

relationshipNotes.note: Referenced by Catalog_ApiRegistry.request_schema and response_schema. Multiple endpoints may share the same schema. Join on spec_version + schema_name.

Properties with ref_schema values reference other schemas in the same table. This creates a graph of schema relationships. For example, AREventRM has properties referencing ActionResultCodeRM, ConsumerAccountIdentifierRM, and Consumer_Contact_Log. Following these links recursively reveals the full data structure.

tables.name: Catalog_BDLElementRegistry
tables.description: BDL element catalog containing one row per element within each entity type. Parsed from XSD schema definition files. Element names correspond directly to DM database column names in crs5_oltp. Excludes nullify_fields structural elements. Child table of Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry via foreign key on spec_version and type_name.
tables.dataFlow: Base structure populated by the BDL XSD parsing script. Enrichment columns (table_column, lookup_table, is_not_nullifiable, is_primary_id, field_description) populated by a separate enrichment script that parses the BDL Import/Export Interface Definition Excel workbook and matches rows to XSD elements by element name overlap scoring. Consumed by modules that perform vendor file column-mapping and BDL XML construction.

columns.name: element_id
columns.description: Identity primary key.

columns.name: format_id
columns.description: FK to Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry.format_id. Integer-based link to the parent entity type, replacing the composite (spec_version, type_name) join. The spec_version and type_name columns are retained as informational context.

columns.name: spec_version
columns.description: XSD spec version identifier. Foreign key to Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry.

columns.name: type_name
columns.description: Parent entity complexType name. Foreign key to Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry.

columns.name: element_name
columns.description: XSD element name. For data entities, corresponds to the DM database column name. For container types, references child entity type names.

columns.name: display_name
columns.description: Human-readable field name for display in the column mapping UI. Shown alongside or in place of the technical element_name to help users identify fields without needing to know DM column names. NULL values fall back to element_name display.

columns.name: data_type
columns.description: Data type with xs: prefix stripped. Primitive types: string, long, decimal, dateTime, int, short, boolean, date. Non-primitive values reference other BDL complexType names for container/child entity relationships.

columns.name: is_required
columns.description: Whether the element is required per XSD minOccurs. Note: XSD requirements are structural minimums — practical import requirements are determined by DM business logic and may differ.

columns.name: is_collection
columns.description: Whether the element has maxOccurs="unbounded", indicating it is a child entity reference that can appear multiple times in the XML. Primarily found on container types in bdl_import_export.xsd.

columns.name: max_length
columns.description: Maximum string length from XSD maxLength restriction. NULL for non-string types or when no restriction is specified.

columns.name: table_column
columns.description: DM database column name from the Excel interface definition. NULL when not enriched or when the table column matches the element name. Only populated when the names differ.

columns.name: lookup_table
columns.description: Reference table containing valid values for this element. From the Excel interface definition Look Up Table column.

columns.name: is_not_nullifiable
columns.description: Whether this element cannot be included in nullify_fields during BDL update operations. Sourced from the Excel Not Nullifiable Columns sheets. Elements marked not-nullifiable are typically identifiers or required business fields.

columns.name: is_primary_id
columns.description: Whether this element is a system-generated primary key identifier. These are auto-assigned by DM and not user-supplied during import. Sourced from the Excel Not Nullifiable Columns Primary ID column.

columns.name: is_visible
columns.description: Whether this element is shown to users in the BDL Import column mapping UI. 1 = visible (default), 0 = hidden. System-generated fields, unreliable identifiers, and internal DM fields should be hidden to prevent user errors. Does not affect the catalog's completeness — hidden elements remain in the spec but are excluded from the import picker.

columns.name: is_import_required
columns.description: Whether this element must be mapped for a BDL import to succeed. 0 = optional (default), 1 = required. Reflects practical DM import requirements as determined by operational experience, distinct from is_required (XSD structural minimum) and is_not_nullifiable (cannot be cleared on update). Populated based on team review of each entity type.

columns.name: field_description
columns.description: Human-readable field description from the Excel interface definition. Supplements the XSD structural data with business context.

columns.name: sort_order
columns.description: Ordinal position of this element within its parent entity. 1-based.

designNotes.note: This table combines data from two sources: the XSD schema definitions (element_name, data_type, is_required, is_collection, max_length) and the Excel interface definition (table_column, lookup_table, is_not_nullifiable, is_primary_id, field_description). XSD data covers all elements. Excel enrichment covers the data entity fields but not wrapper/container references.

The is_required column reflects XSD minOccurs and is almost always 0 (false) — the XSD is permissive by design. The is_not_nullifiable column from the Excel reflects practical import requirements: fields that must have values for the import to succeed, or that cannot be cleared via nullify_fields. These are the fields that matter for building import files.

Elements with is_collection = 1 are child entity references in wrapper/container types. Their data_type values are BDL complexType names (e.g., bdl_cnsmr_phn_data_type) rather than primitive types. These elements define the BDL XML hierarchy — which entities can appear within each operational transaction. Query them to discover valid entity nesting.

The table_column enrichment column is only populated when the DM database column name differs from the BDL XML element name. When NULL, the element_name is the column name. Common differences include reference code fields where the XML uses a _val_txt suffix but the database column uses a _cd suffix.

queries.name: Entity fields with enrichment data
queries.description: Shows all elements for a BDL entity including Excel enrichment. Replace entity_type value as needed.
queries.sql:
SELECT e.element_name, e.data_type, e.is_required, e.max_length,
    e.table_column, e.lookup_table, e.is_not_nullifiable, e.is_primary_id,
    LEFT(e.field_description, 120) AS description_preview
FROM Tools.Catalog_BDLElementRegistry e
INNER JOIN Tools.Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry f
    ON e.spec_version = f.spec_version AND e.type_name = f.type_name
WHERE f.entity_type = 'PHONE'
    AND f.spec_version = '11.1.0.1.6'
ORDER BY e.sort_order;

queries.name: Required fields for BDL import
queries.description: Shows fields that are practically required for import (not-nullifiable or primary IDs). This is the foundation for the column-mapping UI validation.
queries.sql:
SELECT f.entity_type, e.element_name, e.is_not_nullifiable, e.is_primary_id,
    e.lookup_table, LEFT(e.field_description, 100) AS description_preview
FROM Tools.Catalog_BDLElementRegistry e
INNER JOIN Tools.Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry f
    ON e.spec_version = f.spec_version AND e.type_name = f.type_name
WHERE (e.is_not_nullifiable = 1 OR e.is_primary_id = 1)
    AND f.spec_version = '11.1.0.1.6'
ORDER BY f.entity_type, e.sort_order;

queries.name: Fields with lookup table references
queries.description: Shows elements that reference DM lookup tables for valid values. Useful for building validation dropdowns in the import UI.
queries.sql:
SELECT f.entity_type, e.element_name, e.lookup_table,
    LEFT(e.field_description, 100) AS description_preview
FROM Tools.Catalog_BDLElementRegistry e
INNER JOIN Tools.Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry f
    ON e.spec_version = f.spec_version AND e.type_name = f.type_name
WHERE e.lookup_table IS NOT NULL
    AND f.spec_version = '11.1.0.1.6'
ORDER BY f.entity_type, e.sort_order;

queries.name: Discover BDL XML structure for an entity
queries.description: Shows the full path from operational transaction wrapper to data entity, then lists the entity fields. This is the query pattern for constructing BDL import XML.
queries.sql:
DECLARE @entity_type VARCHAR(60) = 'PHONE';
DECLARE @spec VARCHAR(30) = '11.1.0.1.6';

-- Step 1: Find which wrapper contains this entity
SELECT 'Wrapper' AS step, w.type_name AS wrapper, we.element_name AS entity_ref, we.data_type
FROM Tools.Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry w
INNER JOIN Tools.Catalog_BDLElementRegistry we
    ON w.spec_version = we.spec_version AND w.type_name = we.type_name
INNER JOIN Tools.Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry f
    ON we.data_type = f.type_name AND f.spec_version = we.spec_version
WHERE f.entity_type = @entity_type AND w.spec_version = @spec;

-- Step 2: List the entity fields
SELECT 'Field' AS step, e.element_name, e.data_type, e.is_not_nullifiable, e.is_primary_id,
    e.lookup_table, e.max_length
FROM Tools.Catalog_BDLElementRegistry e
INNER JOIN Tools.Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry f
    ON e.spec_version = f.spec_version AND e.type_name = f.type_name
WHERE f.entity_type = @entity_type AND f.spec_version = @spec
ORDER BY e.sort_order;

relationshipNotes.note: Child table. Each element belongs to exactly one format entity via foreign key on spec_version + type_name.

tables.name: Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry
tables.description: BDL bulk data load format catalog containing one row per entity type. Parsed from XSD schema definition files. Each entity type represents a DM data entity that can be imported via BDL. Parent table for Catalog_BDLElementRegistry.
tables.dataFlow: Populated by a Python parsing script that reads BDL XSD schema definition files from DM release packages. Each row represents a BDL data entity that can be imported via bulk data load. Consumed by modules that perform BDL import automation — vendor file upload, column mapping, and XML construction.

columns.name: format_id
columns.description: Identity primary key.

columns.name: spec_version
columns.description: XSD spec version identifier. No default constraint — every insert must explicitly specify.

columns.name: entity_type
columns.description: Entity type name from the XSD fixed type attribute. NULL for wrapper, container, and utility types defined in bdl_import_export.xsd.

columns.name: type_name
columns.description: XSD complexType name. This is the join key to Catalog_BDLElementRegistry.

columns.name: xsd_filename
columns.description: Source XSD filename.

columns.name: folder
columns.description: Subdirectory path within the BDL XSD folder. Indicates the entity hierarchy: consumer, consumer/account, payment/settlement, etc. NULL for root-level files.

columns.name: element_count
columns.description: Number of data elements defined in this entity type. Excludes the nullify_fields structural element.

columns.name: has_parent_ref
columns.description: Whether this entity has a bdl_parent_id attribute indicating a parent-child relationship.

columns.name: has_nullify_fields
columns.description: Whether this entity supports field nullification via the nullify_fields element. A BDL-specific feature for clearing field values on update.

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Whether this entity type is active and available for use. 1 = active (default), 0 = deactivated. Deactivated entities do not appear in entity selection for any user including admin. Deactivation cascades naturally through query filtering — AccessConfig and AccessFieldConfig rows referencing a deactivated entity become unreachable without requiring updates to those tables.

designNotes.note: The folder column captures the subdirectory path within the BDL XSD folder (consumer, consumer/account, payment/settlement, etc.). This provides an at-a-glance view of the entity hierarchy without needing to parse the wrapper types in bdl_import_export.xsd.

The format registry includes wrapper types from bdl_import_export.xsd (e.g., consumer_operational_transaction_data_type, account_operational_transaction_data_type) that have NULL entity_type. These define which data entities belong to each BDL operational transaction. Their child elements in the element registry have is_collection = 1 and data_type values referencing the actual data entity type names.

Entities with has_nullify_fields = 1 support the BDL nullification mechanism — a way to explicitly clear field values during update operations. The nullify_fields structural element itself is excluded from the element registry since it is not a data element.

queries.name: All BDL data entities
queries.description: Lists importable BDL entity types excluding wrapper/container types.
queries.sql:
SELECT entity_type, type_name, folder, element_count, has_parent_ref, has_nullify_fields
FROM Tools.Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry
WHERE spec_version = '11.1.0.1.6'
    AND entity_type IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY folder, entity_type;

queries.name: BDL operational transaction structure
queries.description: Shows which data entities belong to each operational transaction type by querying the wrapper container elements.
queries.sql:
SELECT f.type_name AS container, e.element_name AS entity_ref, e.data_type AS entity_type_name
FROM Tools.Catalog_BDLFormatRegistry f
INNER JOIN Tools.Catalog_BDLElementRegistry e
    ON f.spec_version = e.spec_version AND f.type_name = e.type_name
WHERE f.entity_type IS NULL
    AND f.folder IS NULL
    AND e.is_collection = 1
    AND f.spec_version = '11.1.0.1.6'
ORDER BY f.type_name, e.sort_order;

relationshipNotes.note: Parent table. Each format has zero or more elements in Catalog_BDLElementRegistry linked by foreign key on spec_version + type_name.

tables.name: Catalog_CDLElementRegistry
tables.description: CDL element catalog containing one row per element within each entity type. Parsed from XSD schema definition files. Element names correspond directly to DM database column names in crs5_oltp. Child table of Catalog_CDLFormatRegistry via foreign key on spec_version and type_name.
tables.dataFlow: Populated alongside Catalog_CDLFormatRegistry by the CDL XSD parsing script. Element names correspond directly to DM database column names in crs5_oltp. Consumed by modules that need to discover available fields for CDL import/export operations.

columns.name: element_id
columns.description: Identity primary key.

columns.name: format_id
columns.description: FK to Catalog_CDLFormatRegistry.format_id. Integer-based link to the parent entity type, replacing the composite (spec_version, type_name) join. The spec_version and type_name columns are retained as informational context.

columns.name: spec_version
columns.description: XSD spec version identifier. Foreign key to Catalog_CDLFormatRegistry.

columns.name: type_name
columns.description: Parent entity complexType name. Foreign key to Catalog_CDLFormatRegistry.

columns.name: element_name
columns.description: XSD element name. Corresponds to the DM database column name.

columns.name: display_name
columns.description: Human-readable field name for display in the column mapping UI. Shown alongside or in place of the technical element_name to help users identify fields without needing to know DM column names. NULL values fall back to element_name display.

columns.name: data_type
columns.description: XSD data type with xs: prefix stripped. Common values: string, boolean, int, long, decimal, dateTime, short.

columns.name: is_required
columns.description: Whether the element is required per XSD minOccurs. Derived from minOccurs: 0 means optional, 1 or unspecified means required.

columns.name: is_visible
columns.description: Whether this element is shown to users in import/export UIs. 1 = visible (default), 0 = hidden. Mirrors the Catalog_BDLElementRegistry pattern for consistency across catalog tables.

columns.name: is_import_required
columns.description: Whether this element must be mapped for a CDL import to succeed. 0 = optional (default), 1 = required. Mirrors the Catalog_BDLElementRegistry pattern for consistency across catalog tables.

columns.name: max_length
columns.description: Maximum string length from XSD maxLength restriction. NULL for non-string types or when no restriction is specified.

columns.name: sort_order
columns.description: Ordinal position of this element within its parent entity. 1-based.

designNotes.note: CDL element names map directly to crs5_oltp database column names. This is a key difference from BDL, where element names sometimes differ from table column names. CDL elements can be used directly for cross-referencing with the DM OLTP schema.

queries.name: Entity fields with types
queries.description: Shows all elements for a given CDL entity type. Replace entity_type value as needed.
queries.sql:
SELECT e.element_name, e.data_type, e.is_required, e.max_length
FROM Tools.Catalog_CDLElementRegistry e
INNER JOIN Tools.Catalog_CDLFormatRegistry f
    ON e.spec_version = f.spec_version AND e.type_name = f.type_name
WHERE f.entity_type = 'CREDITOR'
    AND f.spec_version = '11.1.0.1.6'
ORDER BY e.sort_order;

relationshipNotes.note: Child table. Each element belongs to exactly one format entity via foreign key on spec_version + type_name.

tables.name: Catalog_CDLFormatRegistry
tables.description: CDL configuration data format catalog containing one row per entity type. Parsed from XSD schema definition files. Each entity type represents a DM configuration object that can be imported or exported via CDL. Parent table for Catalog_CDLElementRegistry.
tables.dataFlow: Populated by a Python parsing script that reads CDL XSD schema definition files from DM release packages. Each row represents a CDL configuration entity type that can be imported or exported. Consumed by modules that perform CDL import/export automation and configuration management.

columns.name: format_id
columns.description: Identity primary key.

columns.name: spec_version
columns.description: XSD spec version identifier. No default constraint — every insert must explicitly specify.

columns.name: entity_type
columns.description: Entity type name from the XSD fixed type attribute. NULL for sub-types and utility types that lack a type attribute.

columns.name: type_name
columns.description: XSD complexType name. This is the join key to Catalog_CDLElementRegistry.

columns.name: xsd_filename
columns.description: Source XSD filename within the CDL entity folder.

columns.name: element_count
columns.description: Number of data elements defined in this entity type.

columns.name: has_parent_ref
columns.description: Whether this entity has a cdl_item_stgng_prnt_id attribute indicating a parent-child relationship.

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Whether this entity type is active and available for use. 1 = active (default), 0 = deactivated. Deactivated entities do not appear in entity selection for any user including admin.

designNotes.note: Most entities have an entity_type value from the XSD fixed type attribute (e.g., CREDITOR, ACTIONCODE). Some entries have NULL entity_type — these are sub-types, utility types, or abstract base types defined in shared XSD files like cdl_udp_field_type.xsd. The type_name column is always populated and serves as the primary identifier.

Entities with has_parent_ref = 1 have a cdl_item_stgng_prnt_id attribute in the XSD, indicating they are child entities that reference a parent during import. For example, CREDITOR_CONFIGURATION is a child of CREDITOR. The parent relationship is not explicitly stored in this table — it is implied by the CDL import structure and documented in the DM interface definition.

queries.name: All CDL entity types
queries.description: Lists all CDL configuration entity types with their element counts and parent reference status.
queries.sql:
SELECT entity_type, type_name, element_count, has_parent_ref, xsd_filename
FROM Tools.Catalog_CDLFormatRegistry
WHERE spec_version = '11.1.0.1.6'
    AND entity_type IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY entity_type;

relationshipNotes.note: Parent table. Each format has zero or more elements in Catalog_CDLElementRegistry linked by foreign key on spec_version + type_name.

tables.name: ServerConfig
tables.description: Per-server configuration for Tools module operations. One row per tools-enabled target server, providing DM API connection details and dmfs file import paths. Child of dbo.ServerRegistry via server_id foreign key.
tables.dataFlow: Rows are manually inserted when a server is designated as a Tools operations target (tools_enabled = 1 on ServerRegistry). The BDL Import workflow reads this table to resolve the target server's API URL and dmfs file path based on the user's environment selection. Future Tools pipelines (Payment, CDL, New Business) consume the same rows using their respective folder columns.

columns.name: config_id
columns.description: Identity primary key.

columns.name: server_id
columns.description: FK to dbo.ServerRegistry. Identifies the tools-enabled server that owns the dmfs file share for this environment.

columns.name: server_name
columns.description: Denormalized server hostname from ServerRegistry. Avoids joins for common queries.

columns.name: environment
columns.description: Denormalized environment from ServerRegistry (PROD, STAGE, TEST). Used as the primary lookup key when the UI environment selector resolves to a server configuration.

columns.name: db_instance
columns.description: SQL Server instance name or AG listener for the crs5_oltp database in this environment. Used by validation and query operations that need to read DM reference tables. For standalone environments (TEST), this is the same as server_name. For AG environments (PROD, STAGE), this is the AG listener name.

columns.name: api_base_url
columns.description: Full base URL for the DM REST API. Includes protocol, hostname, port, and base path (e.g., http://dm-prod-app:8080/dm-rest-services/api). May point to a different server than the dmfs path in multi-node environments. API endpoint paths are appended to this value.

columns.name: dmfs_base_path
columns.description: Base UNC path for the DM file system import folder (e.g., \\\\dm-prod-app3\\e$\\dmfs\\import). Pipeline-specific subfolder columns are appended to construct full paths.

columns.name: dmfs_bdl_folder
columns.description: Subfolder name under dmfs_base_path for BDL import files. Default: bdl.

columns.name: dmfs_cdl_folder
columns.description: Subfolder name under dmfs_base_path for CDL import files. Default: cdl.

columns.name: dmfs_payment_folder
columns.description: Subfolder name under dmfs_base_path for payment import files. Default: payments.

columns.name: dmfs_newbusiness_folder
columns.description: Subfolder name under dmfs_base_path for new business import files. Default: newbusiness.

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Whether this server configuration is active. 0 = disabled, 1 = enabled (default).

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When the configuration was created.

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who created the configuration.

columns.name: modified_dttm
columns.description: When the configuration was last modified.

columns.name: modified_by
columns.description: Who last modified the configuration.

designNotes.note: server_name and environment are stored directly alongside the server_id foreign key. This avoids joins to ServerRegistry for the most common query pattern: selecting configuration by environment.

Full file paths are constructed by combining dmfs_base_path with the pipeline-specific folder column and a trailing backslash. For example, a BDL import path is dmfs_base_path + backslash + dmfs_bdl_folder + backslash. The folder columns have sensible defaults but are stored explicitly so paths remain configurable without code changes.

In multi-node environments (Production, Staging), the dmfs file path and the API base URL may point to different servers. Files are written to the ETL/content server (APP3) via UNC share, while API calls to trigger imports are fired against the domain controller (APP). The executing server reads the file from APP3 via the shared dmfs mount.

queries.name: Configuration by environment
queries.description: Returns the full server configuration for a given environment. Primary query pattern for all Tools operations.
queries.sql:
SELECT server_name, environment, api_base_url,
    dmfs_base_path, dmfs_bdl_folder, dmfs_cdl_folder,
    dmfs_payment_folder, dmfs_newbusiness_folder
FROM Tools.ServerConfig
WHERE environment = 'PROD'
    AND is_active = 1;

relationshipNotes.note: Child table. server_id references ServerRegistry.server_id. Only servers with tools_enabled = 1 should have rows in this table. The unique constraint on server_id enforces a 1:1 relationship.

procedures.name: sp_SyncColumnOrdinals
procedures.description: Aligns Object_Metadata column description sort_order values with actual sys.columns column_id ordinals. Compares active column description rows against the system catalog, updates misaligned sort_order values to match current column positions, and deactivates description rows for columns that no longer exist. Supports three scopes: single table (both parameters provided), all tables in a schema (@SchemaName only), or full database (no parameters). Returns a detail result set in single-table preview mode and a per-table summary in all other modes. Runs in preview mode by default.

designNotes.note: @PreviewOnly defaults to 1, requiring an explicit opt-in to apply changes. The preview output shows every proposed action (UPDATE or DEACTIVATE) with before and after sort_order values, plus a summary of aligned, misaligned, orphaned, and missing columns. This makes it safe to run exploratively against any table without risk.

designNotes.summary: Detects and deactivates metadata for dropped columns.
designNotes.note: When a column exists in Object_Metadata but not in sys.columns, the row is deactivated (is_active = 0) and sort_order is set to 0 rather than deleted. This preserves the documentation history while removing the orphan from active exports and reference pages.

designNotes.summary: Reports columns that lack documentation but does not create rows.
designNotes.note: Columns found in sys.columns with no matching active Object_Metadata description row are reported in the output as informational. The proc does not auto-generate description rows — that remains a manual enrichment task to ensure content quality.

relationshipNotes.note: Reads and updates dbo.Object_Metadata rows where property_type is description and column_name is populated. Only active rows (is_active = 1) are evaluated. Sort_order updates and deactivations set modified_dttm and modified_by via GETDATE() and SUSER_SNAME().
